title: jingyaogong/minimind
friendlyTitle: MiniMind teaches how to train a tiny language model
summary: MiniMind is a compact educational LLM project that shows how to train a roughly 64M-parameter model from scratch, with code covering pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, LoRA, RLHF-style methods, tool use, agentic RL, distillation, and related workflows. The repository is highly starred and appeared prominently in today’s AI-related GitHub candidates.
friendlySummary: MiniMind shows how to build a small LLM from scratch and includes many parts of the modern training process.
whyItMatters: It is useful for learning the full LLM training pipeline without needing frontier-scale hardware. Students, engineers, and small teams can use it to understand model internals, data preparation, post-training, and agentic extensions in a reproducible small-model setting.
friendlyWhyItMatters: It is a good learning project if you want to understand how language models are built without needing huge computers.
